author: The Lee Fam 🦖
title: TLDR; Get the Baby Brezza Air Swirl and not the Philips Avent one. No mess and heats the bottle to the perfect temp every time 😌 ALSO if you are planning to use Dr. Brown’s bottles - the tall ones do not fit in Philips Avent !  If you’re expecting or a new parent, this is for you! A bottle warmer can make feeding time so much easier. Whether you’re warming breast milk or formula, getting the temperature just right makes a BIG difference. We started with the Philips Avent Bottle Warmer. It did the job, but since it uses water to heat bottles, we often ended up with overheated milk and a bit of a mess—water would drip everywhere when we took the bottle out. The dial is based on how much liquid is in the bottle, but we found it pretty inconsistent. There was no timer or indicator when it was done so it would often overheat the bottle. Plus, it didn’t quite fit taller bottles like the 8oz Dr. Brown’s. 🤔 Then we came across the Baby Brezza Air Swirl and honestly, it’s been a game changer. It uses circulating hot air (no water!), which means more even heating and no hot spots. It fits our tall bottles, has a super clear timer guide, and gets the temp just right (with a little trial and error). It’s a bit noisier and needs a flat surface, but we’re totally fine with that trade-off. There are a bunch of options out there, but if you’re choosing between water and air-based warmers, we’re definitely team hot air! 🔥💨 #thisnotthat #newparenttips #parenthacks #babygear #bottlewarmer #babyregistry
